name: Smoke-at-Home Brisket
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the mesquite chips in water for 4 hours.
2. Prepare a charcoal or oakwood fire in the smoker and let it burn for 1 hour, or until flames disappear.
3. Drain the chips and place them on the coals.
4. Place a water pan in the smoker and add water to the pan to the depth of the fill line.
5. Sprinkle the brisket with salt and pepper, then place it on the food rack.
6. Cover the brisket with the smoker lid.
7. Cook for 8 to 9 hours, or until an instant-read thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the meat registers 180°F (82°C).
8. Add more charcoal, chips, and water as needed throughout the cooking process.
9. The brisket yields 15 to 18 servings.
Source: Southern Living, September 1995 and Kevin Jones, pit cook for the County Line (On the Lake) in Austin. He says the secret to good brisket is slow cooking.
